exampleSentences: Em cám ơn anh rất nhiều.
Anh cám ơn chị nhiều lắm.
Chị cám ơn em nhiều nhé.
notes:
cám ơn means “thank you.” You use it to show gratitude when someone helps you, gives you something, or does something nice.

It’s a verb, so you can say it directly to someone or add a reason.  

You can make it stronger with words like rất nhiều (very much). The more standard spelling is cảm ơn — same meaning, just different spelling.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + cám ơn (thank you) + anh (you - older male) + rất (very) + nhiều (much)
Anh (I) + cám ơn (thank you) + chị (you - older female) + nhiều (much) + lắm (so)
Chị (I) + cám ơn (thank you) + em (you - younger) + nhiều (much) + nhé (softener)
notes_md:
**cám ơn** means “thank you.” You use it to show gratitude when someone helps you, gives you something, or does something nice.

It’s a verb, so you can say it directly to someone or add a reason.  

You can make it stronger with words like *rất nhiều* (very much). The more standard spelling is **cảm ơn** — same meaning, just different spelling.

example.vi: Cám ơn bạn đã giúp đỡ.

definitions.vi: Bày tỏ lòng biết ơn hoặc sự trân trọng.
